blinded.name: Blinded
blinded.description: Can't see; automatically fails ability checks that require sight. Attack rolls against it have advantage, and its own attack rolls have disadvantage.

charmed.name: Charmed
charmed.description: Can't attack the charmer or target the charmer with harmful abilities or magical effects; the charmer has advantage on social ability checks with it. (Adjudicated by the GM; no roll modifiers.)

deafened.name: Deafened
deafened.description: Can't hear; automatically fails ability checks that require hearing. (Adjudicated by the GM; no roll modifiers.)

frightened.name: Frightened
frightened.description: Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls while the source of its fear is within line of sight; can't willingly move closer to the source.

grappled.name: Grappled
grappled.description: Speed 0; ends when the grappler is incapacitated or the target is removed from the grappler's reach. (No movement model; adjudicated by the GM.)

incapacitated.name: Incapacitated
incapacitated.description: Can't take actions, bonus actions, or reactions; loses its turns.

invisible.name: Invisible
invisible.description: Can't be seen without special sense; its attack rolls have advantage, and attack rolls against it have disadvantage.

paralyzed.name: Paralyzed
paralyzed.description: Incapacitated and can't move or speak; loses its turns. Automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws. Attack rolls against it have advantage (a hit from within 5 ft. is a critical hit — no positioning model, adjudicated by the GM).

petrified.name: Petrified
petrified.description: Transformed to stone: incapacitated, can't move or speak, unaware of its surroundings; loses its turns. Automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws; attack rolls against it have advantage; resistant to all damage and immune to poison (damage resistance not modeled per-effect — adjudicated by the GM).

poisoned.name: Poisoned
poisoned.description: Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

prone.name: Prone
prone.description: Disadvantage on attack rolls; attacks against have advantage; auto-stands at turn start. (SRD: attackers have advantage within 5 ft., disadvantage from farther away — no positioning model, so advantage applies flatly.)

restrained.name: Restrained
restrained.description: Speed 0. Attack rolls against it have advantage, and its own attack rolls and Dexterity saving throws have disadvantage. (DEX-save disadvantage not modeled; adjudicated by the GM.)

stunned.name: Stunned
stunned.description: Incapacitated, can't move, speaks only falteringly; loses its turns. Automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws; attack rolls against it have advantage.

unconscious.name: Unconscious
unconscious.description: Incapacitated, prone, can't move or speak, unaware of its surroundings; loses its turns and drops what it's holding. Automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saving throws; attack rolls against it have advantage (a hit from within 5 ft. is a critical hit — adjudicated by the GM).

exhaustion-1.name: Exhaustion (Level 1)
exhaustion-1.description: Exhaustion level 1: -2 on all d20 tests (attack rolls, ability checks, saving throws). SRD: speed also reduced; death at level 6 (adjudicated by the GM).

exhaustion-2.name: Exhaustion (Level 2)
exhaustion-2.description: Exhaustion level 2: -4 on all d20 tests. SRD: speed also reduced; death at level 6 (adjudicated by the GM).

exhaustion-3.name: Exhaustion (Level 3)
exhaustion-3.description: Exhaustion level 3: -6 on all d20 tests. SRD: speed also reduced; death at level 6 (adjudicated by the GM).

exhaustion-4.name: Exhaustion (Level 4)
exhaustion-4.description: Exhaustion level 4: -8 on all d20 tests. SRD: speed also reduced; death at level 6 (adjudicated by the GM).

exhaustion-5.name: Exhaustion (Level 5)
exhaustion-5.description: Exhaustion level 5: -10 on all d20 tests. SRD: speed also reduced; death at level 6 (adjudicated by the GM).

exhaustion-6.name: Exhaustion (Level 6)
exhaustion-6.description: Exhaustion level 6: -12 on all d20 tests. SRD: a creature dies at exhaustion level 6 (adjudicated by the GM).
